problem text: At a particular busy intersection, the likelihood of experiencing at least one vehicle accident in a $1$ hour period is $\dfrac{8}{9}$. Given that vehicle accidents happen independently and at a uniform rate over time, determine the probability of having at least one vehicle accident in a $30$ minute period.
problem solution: Let the sought probability be $p$. We know that the probability of no vehicle accident occurring in the hour is $\dfrac{1}{9}$ by using the complement. This is equivalent to having no vehicle accident in each of the two periods made up of the first and second $30$ minutes of the hour. As per the problem, the number of vehicle accidents in separate intervals are independent because they occur at a consistent rate over time and each event is independent. The likelihood of no vehicle accident in each of these intervals is $1-p$, hence the probability of no vehicle accident in both intervals is $(1-p)^2$. Therefore, $(1-p)^2 = \dfrac{1}{9}$, which implies $p = \dfrac{2}{3}$.
problem hint: Divide the hour into two separate $30$-minute periods. If there is no vehicle accident in the hour, then there is no accident in each of the independent $30$-minute periods.
